revision.text: #redirect[[en:Prince_Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{unicode}} '''天足彦国押人命'''(あまのたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと;生没年不詳)は日本の皇族。第5代[[孝昭天皇]]皇子で弟に日本足彦国押人尊即ち第6代[[孝安天皇]]がいる。母は[[天忍男命]]の娘で瀛津世襲命（奥津余曽）の妹、皇后・世襲足媛命（余曽多本毘売命、日置姫とも。）。[[古事記]]では'''天押帯日子命'''と記されている。天足彦国押人命には[[押彦命]]・押媛命の2人の子がおり、押媛命は孝安天皇の妃となる。 古事記では阿那臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・大宅臣（おおやけのおみ）・小野臣・柿本臣・春日臣・栗田臣・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣（むぎのおみ）・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君・壱師君・近淡海国造（ちかつあふみ）の祖とされ、[[日本書紀]]に和珥臣の祖、[[新撰姓氏録]]に猪甘部首の祖であるとされる。 [[奈良県]][[平群町]]にある[[猪上神社]]の祭神であり、[[天理市]]・[[和爾下神社]]も明治初年頃は祭神としていた。 {{history-stub}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族|あまのたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと]] [[Category:日本神話の人物|あまたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと]]

'''天足彦国押人命'''(あまのたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと;生没年不詳)は日本の皇族。第5代[[孝昭天皇]]皇子で弟に日本足彦国押人尊即ち第6代[[孝安天皇]]がいる。母は[[天忍男命]]の娘で瀛津世襲命（奥津余曽）の妹、皇后・世襲足媛命（余曽多本毘売命、日置姫とも。）。[[古事記]]では'''天押帯日子命'''と記されている。天足彦国押人命には[[押彦命]]・押媛命の2人の子がおり、押媛命は孝安天皇の妃となる。
古事記では阿那臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・大宅臣（おおやけのおみ）・小野臣・柿本臣・春日臣・栗田臣・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣（むぎのおみ）・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君・壱師君・近淡海国造（ちかつあふみ）の祖とされ、[[日本書紀]]に和珥臣の祖、[[新撰姓氏録]]に猪甘部首の祖であるとされる。
[[奈良県]][[平群町]]にある[[猪上神社]]の祭神であり、[[天理市]]・[[和爾下神社]]も明治初年頃は祭神としていた。
{{history-stub}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族|あまのたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと]] [[Category:日本神話の人物|あまたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと]]

'''天足彦国押人命'''（あまたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと、生没年不詳）は、『[[古事記]]』『[[日本書紀]]』に記される皇族（王族）。 '''天押帯日子命'''とも。記紀によれば、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子で、母は瀛津世襲の妹・世襲足媛命（よそたらしひめ、余曽多本毘売命・日置姫）。同母弟に日本足彦国押人尊（[[孝安天皇]]）がおり、その皇后・押媛命は天足彦の娘と推定される。事績は伝わらないが、『古事記』には、阿那臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・大宅臣（おおやけのおみ）・[[小野氏|小野臣]]・柿本臣・春日臣・粟田臣・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣（むさのおみ）・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君・壱師君・[[近江国|近淡海]]国造（ちかつあふみ）の祖、『[[日本書紀]]』に[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]の祖とし、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』にも同様の系譜記載がある。なお、子に和邇日子押人命（稚押彦命）がおり（『和邇系図』）、名からして和邇氏族の宗祖的存在と言える。
[[奈良県]][[平群町]]にある[[猪上神社]]の祭神であり、[[天理市]]・[[和爾下神社]]も明治初年頃は祭神としていた。
{{history-stub}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族|あまのたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと]] [[Category:日本神話の人物|あまたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと]]

[[奈良県]][[平群町]]にある[[猪上神社]]の祭神であり、[[天理市]]・[[和爾下神社]]も明治初年頃は祭神としていた。

{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 天足彦国押人命
（あまたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = [[孝昭天皇]]の第 1[[皇子]] | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不明 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不明 | 没地 = 不明 | 埋葬日 = 不明 | 埋葬地 = 不明 | 配偶者1 = | 子女 = 和邇日子押人命（稚押彦命） | 父親 = [[孝昭天皇]]（第 5代[[天皇]]） | 母親 = [[世襲足媛]] | 役職 = }} [[Image:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|thumb|right|400px|天皇略系図（初代 - 第10代） [[media:Emperor family tree of Japan 1-10 (ja).svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]] '''天足彦国押人命'''（あまたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと、生没年不詳）は、『[[古事記]]』『[[日本書紀]]』に記される[[皇族]]（王族）。　'''天押帯日子命'''とも。記紀によれば、[[孝昭天皇]]の第 1[[皇子]]で、母は瀛津世襲の妹・[[世襲足媛]]命（よそたらしひめ『日本書紀』本文、『古事記』では余曽多本毘売命、日置姫）。同母弟に日本足彦国押人尊（[[孝安天皇]]）がおり、『日本書紀』本文での皇后・[[押媛]]命は天足彦國押人の娘と記述される。事績は伝わらないが、『古事記』には、阿那臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・大宅臣（おおやけのおみ）・[[小野氏|小野臣]]（のち小野[[朝臣]]）・柿本臣・[[春日氏|春日臣]]（のち春日朝臣）・粟田臣・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣（むさのおみ）・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君・壱師君・[[近江国|近淡海]]国造（ちかつあふみ）の祖、『[[日本書紀]]』に[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]の祖とし、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』にも同様の系譜記載がある。なお、子に和邇日子押人命（稚押彦命）がおり（『和邇系図』）、名からして和邇氏族の宗祖的存在と言える。 [[奈良県]][[平群町]]にある[[猪上神社]]、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]小野（旧：[[滋賀県]]滋賀郡志賀町）の[[小野神社 (大津市)|小野神社]]の祭神である。また[[奈良県]][[天理市]]・[[和爾下神社]]も[[明治]]初年頃は祭神としていた。 == 関連項目 == * [[小野氏]] * [[和珥氏]] * [[春日氏]] * [[大宅氏]] * [[粟田氏]] * [[柿本氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:あまのたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:小野氏|あまのたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと]]

{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 天足彦国押人命

'''天足彦国押人命'''（あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「天足彦国押人命」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''天押帯日子命'''（あめおしたらしひこのみこと{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}）」、他文献では「天足彦国忍人命」とも表記される。『日本書紀』『古事記』とも、事績に関する記載はない。 第5代[[孝昭天皇]][[皇子]]で、第6代[[孝安天皇]]の同母兄、第7代[[孝霊天皇]]の外祖父である{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。[[和珥氏]]（和邇氏/丸邇氏）・[[春日氏]]・[[小野氏]]ら諸氏族の祖とされる。 == 系譜 ==
'''『日本書紀』に基づく関係系図''' {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|02| 01={{Supra|5}}[[孝昭天皇]]|02=[[世襲足媛|{{color|#FC4E6B|世襲足媛}}]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|^|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||!| 01='''天足彦国押人命'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01=[[押媛|{{color|#FC4E6B|押媛}}]]|02={{Supra|6}}[[孝安天皇]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}}[[孝霊天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}}
{{smaller|（名称は『日本書紀』を第一とし、括弧内に『古事記』ほかを記載）}} 『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』によれば、第5代[[孝昭天皇]]と、[[世襲足媛]]（よそたらしひめ、余曽多本毘売命）との間に生まれた第一[[皇子]]である。同母弟には日本足彦国押人天皇（大倭帯日子国押人命、第6代[[孝安天皇]]）がいる。 子に関して、『日本書紀』では娘の[[押媛]]が孝安天皇の[[皇后]]となり、[[孝霊天皇]]（第7代）を産んだとする。また『和邇系図』では、和邇日子押人命（稚押彦命）の名を挙げる。 == 後裔氏族 == 『日本書紀』では天押帯日子命（天足彦国押人命）について、[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]（和珥氏）の祖とする{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『古事記』では、[[春日氏|春日臣]]・大宅臣・粟田臣・[[小野氏|小野臣]]・柿本臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・阿那臣・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君・壱師君・[[近淡海国造]]ら諸氏族の祖としている{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 - [[春日氏|大春日朝臣]]・吉田連・丈部 * 右京皇別 - 栗田朝臣・山上朝臣・真野臣・和邇部・安那公・野中 * 山城国皇別 - [[小野氏|小野朝臣]]・粟田朝臣・小野臣・和邇部・大宅・村公・度守首 * 大和国皇別 - 柿下朝臣・布留宿禰・久米臣 * 摂津国皇別 - 和邇部・羽束首 * 河内国皇別 - 大宅臣・壬生臣・物部 * 和泉国皇別 - 葦占臣・物部・網部物部・根連・櫛代造 * 右京未定雑姓 - 中臣臣 * 和泉国未定雑姓 - 猪甘首 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=天押帯日子命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[小野氏]] * [[和珥氏]] * [[春日氏]] * [[猪上神社]] （[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]） * [[小野神社 (大津市)|小野神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]小野） {{DEFAULTSORT:あめたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:小野氏]] [[Category:和珥氏]]

revision.text:
'''天足彦国押人命'''（あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「天足彦国押人命」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''天押帯日子命'''（あめおしたらしひこのみこと{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}）」、他文献では「天足彦国忍人命」とも表記される。『日本書紀』『古事記』とも、事績に関する記載はない。 第5代[[孝昭天皇]][[皇子]]で、第6代[[孝安天皇]]の同母兄、第7代[[孝霊天皇]]の外祖父である{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。[[和珥氏]]（和邇氏/丸邇氏）・[[春日氏]]・[[小野氏]]ら諸氏族の祖とされる。 == 系譜 ==
'''『日本書紀』に基づく関係系図''' {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|02| 01={{Supra|5}}[[孝昭天皇]]|02=[[世襲足媛|{{color|#FC4E6B|世襲足媛}}]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|^|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||!| 01='''天足彦国押人命'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01=[[押媛|{{color|#FC4E6B|押媛}}]]|02={{Supra|6}}[[孝安天皇]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}}[[孝霊天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}}
{{smaller|（名称は『日本書紀』を第一とし、括弧内に『古事記』ほかを記載）}} 『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』によれば、第5代[[孝昭天皇]]と、[[世襲足媛]]（よそたらしひめ、余曽多本毘売命）との間に生まれた第一[[皇子]]である。同母弟には日本足彦国押人天皇（大倭帯日子国押人命、第6代[[孝安天皇]]）がいる。 子に関して、『日本書紀』では娘の[[押媛]]が孝安天皇の[[皇后]]となり、[[孝霊天皇]]（第7代）を産んだとする。また『和邇系図』では、和邇日子押人命（稚押彦命）の名を挙げる。 == 後裔氏族 == 『日本書紀』では天足彦国押人命について、[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]（和珥氏）の祖とする{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『古事記』では、[[春日氏|春日臣]]・大宅臣・粟田臣・[[小野氏|小野臣]]・柿本臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・阿那臣・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君・壱師君・[[近淡海国造]]ら諸氏族の祖としている{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 - [[春日氏|大春日朝臣]]・吉田連・丈部 * 右京皇別 - 栗田朝臣・山上朝臣・真野臣・和邇部・安那公・野中 * 山城国皇別 - [[小野氏|小野朝臣]]・粟田朝臣・小野臣・和邇部・大宅・村公・度守首 * 大和国皇別 - 柿下朝臣・布留宿禰・久米臣 * 摂津国皇別 - 和邇部・羽束首 * 河内国皇別 - 大宅臣・壬生臣・物部 * 和泉国皇別 - 葦占臣・物部・網部物部・根連・櫛代造 * 右京未定雑姓 - 中臣臣 * 和泉国未定雑姓 - 猪甘首 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=天押帯日子命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[小野氏]] * [[和珥氏]] * [[春日氏]] * [[猪上神社]] （[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]） * [[小野神社 (大津市)|小野神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]小野） {{DEFAULTSORT:あめたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:小野氏]] [[Category:和珥氏]]

'''天足彦国押人命'''（あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「天足彦国押人命」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''天押帯日子命'''（あめおしたらしひこのみこと{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}）」、他文献では「天足彦国忍人命」とも表記される。『日本書紀』『古事記』とも、事績に関する記載はない。 第5代[[孝昭天皇]][[皇子]]で、第6代[[孝安天皇]]の同母兄、第7代[[孝霊天皇]]の外祖父である{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。[[和珥氏]]（和邇氏/丸邇氏）・[[春日氏]]・[[小野氏]]ら諸氏族の祖とされる。 == 系譜 ==

{{smaller|（名称は『日本書紀』を第一とし、括弧内に『古事記』ほかを記載）}} 『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』によれば、第5代[[孝昭天皇]]と、[[世襲足媛]]（よそたらしひめ、余曽多本毘売命）との間に生まれた第一[[皇子]]である。同母弟には日本足彦国押人天皇（大倭帯日子国押人命、第6代[[孝安天皇]]）がいる。 子に関して、『日本書紀』では娘の[[押媛]]が孝安天皇の[[皇后]]となり、[[孝霊天皇]]（第7代）を産んだとする。また『和邇系図』では、和邇日子押人命（稚押彦命）の名を挙げる。 == 後裔氏族 == 『日本書紀』では天足彦国押人命について、[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]（和珥氏）の祖とする{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『古事記』では、[[春日氏|春日臣]]・大宅臣・[[粟田臣]]・[[乙瀬臣]]・[[小野氏|小野臣]]・柿本臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・阿那臣・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君・壱師君・[[近淡海国造]]ら諸氏族の祖としている{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 - [[春日氏|大春日朝臣]]・吉田連・[[丈部]] * 右京皇別 - 栗田朝臣・山上朝臣・真野臣・和邇部・安那公・野中 * 山城国皇別 - [[小野氏|小野朝臣]]・粟田朝臣・小野臣・和邇部・大宅・村公・度守首 * 大和国皇別 - 柿下朝臣・布留宿禰・久米臣 * 摂津国皇別 - 和邇部・羽束首 * 河内国皇別 - 大宅臣・壬生臣・物部 * 和泉国皇別 - 葦占臣・物部・網部物部・根連・櫛代造 * 右京未定雑姓 - 中臣臣 * 和泉国未定雑姓 - 猪甘首 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=天押帯日子命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[小野氏]] * [[和珥氏]] * [[春日氏]] * [[猪上神社]] （[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]） * [[小野神社 (大津市)|小野神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]小野） {{DEFAULTSORT:あめたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:小野氏]] [[Category:和珥氏]]

{{smaller|（名称は『日本書紀』を第一とし、括弧内に『古事記』ほかを記載）}} 『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』によれば、第5代[[孝昭天皇]]と、[[世襲足媛]]（よそたらしひめ、余曽多本毘売命）との間に生まれた第一[[皇子]]である。同母弟には日本足彦国押人天皇（大倭帯日子国押人命、第6代[[孝安天皇]]）がいる。 子に関して、『日本書紀』では娘の[[押媛]]が孝安天皇の[[皇后]]となり、[[孝霊天皇]]（第7代）を産んだとする。また『和邇系図』では、和邇日子押人命（稚押彦命）の名を挙げる。 == 後裔氏族 == 『日本書紀』では天足彦国押人命について、[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]（和珥氏）の祖とする{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『古事記』では、[[春日氏|春日臣]]・大宅臣・粟田臣・[[小野氏|小野臣]]・柿本臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・阿那臣・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君・壱師君・[[近淡海国造]]ら諸氏族の祖としている{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 - [[春日氏|大春日朝臣]]・吉田連・[[丈部]] * 右京皇別 - 栗田朝臣・山上朝臣・真野臣・和邇部・安那公・野中 * 山城国皇別 - [[小野氏|小野朝臣]]・粟田朝臣・小野臣・和邇部・大宅・村公・度守首 * 大和国皇別 - 柿下朝臣・布留宿禰・久米臣 * 摂津国皇別 - 和邇部・羽束首 * 河内国皇別 - 大宅臣・壬生臣・物部 * 和泉国皇別 - 葦占臣・物部・網部物部・根連・櫛代造 * 右京未定雑姓 - 中臣臣 * 和泉国未定雑姓 - 猪甘首 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=天押帯日子命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[小野氏]] * [[和珥氏]] * [[春日氏]] * [[猪上神社]] （[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]） * [[小野神社 (大津市)|小野神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]小野） {{DEFAULTSORT:あめたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:小野氏]] [[Category:和珥氏]]

'''天足彦国押人命'''（あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「天足彦国押人命」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''天押帯日子命'''（あめおしたらしひこのみこと{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}）」、他文献では「天足彦国忍人命」とも表記される。『日本書紀』『古事記』とも、事績に関する記載はない。 第5代[[孝昭天皇]][[皇子]]で、第6代[[孝安天皇]]の同母兄、第7代[[孝霊天皇]]の外祖父である{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。[[和珥氏]]（和邇氏/丸邇氏）・[[春日氏]]・[[小野氏]]ら諸氏族の祖とされる。 == 系譜 ==

{{smaller|（名称は『日本書紀』を第一とし、括弧内に『古事記』ほかを記載）}} 『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』によれば、第5代[[孝昭天皇]]と、[[世襲足媛]]（よそたらしひめ、余曽多本毘売命）との間に生まれた第一[[皇子]]である。同母弟には日本足彦国押人天皇（大倭帯日子国押人命、第6代[[孝安天皇]]）がいる。 子に関して、『日本書紀』では娘の[[押媛]]が孝安天皇の[[皇后]]となり、[[孝霊天皇]]（第7代）を産んだとする。また『和邇系図』では、和邇日子押人命（稚押彦命）の名を挙げる。 == 後裔氏族 == 『日本書紀』では天足彦国押人命について、[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]（和珥氏）の祖とする{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『古事記』では、[[春日氏|春日臣]]・大宅臣・[[粟田氏|粟田臣]]・[[小野氏|小野臣]]・柿本臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・阿那臣（吉備穴国造）・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣（[[武社国造]]）・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君（飯高県造）・壱師君（壱師県造）・[[近淡海国造]]ら諸氏族の祖としている{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 - [[春日氏|大春日朝臣]]・吉田連・[[丈部]] * 右京皇別 - 栗田朝臣・山上朝臣・真野臣・和邇部・安那公・野中 * 山城国皇別 - [[小野氏|小野朝臣]]・粟田朝臣・小野臣・和邇部・大宅・村公・度守首 * 大和国皇別 - 柿下朝臣・布留宿禰・[[久米氏|久米臣]] * 摂津国皇別 - 和邇部・羽束首 * 河内国皇別 - 大宅臣・壬生臣・物部 * 和泉国皇別 - 葦占臣・物部・網部物部・根連・櫛代造 * 右京未定雑姓 - 中臣臣 * 和泉国未定雑姓 - 猪甘首 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=天押帯日子命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[小野氏]] * [[和珥氏]] * [[春日氏]] * [[猪上神社]] （[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]） * [[小野神社 (大津市)|小野神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]小野） * [[多利思比孤]] {{DEFAULTSORT:あめたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:小野氏]] [[Category:和珥氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{smaller|（名称は『日本書紀』を第一とし、括弧内に『古事記』ほかを記載）}} 『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』によれば、第5代[[孝昭天皇]]と、[[世襲足媛]]（よそたらしひめ、余曽多本毘売命）との間に生まれた第一[[皇子]]である。同母弟には日本足彦国押人天皇（大倭帯日子国押人命、第6代[[孝安天皇]]）がいる。 子に関して、『日本書紀』では娘の[[押媛]]が孝安天皇の[[皇后]]となり、[[孝霊天皇]]（第7代）を産んだとする。 == 後裔氏族 == 『日本書紀』では天足彦国押人命について、[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]（和珥氏）の祖とする{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『古事記』では、[[春日氏|春日臣]]・大宅臣・[[粟田氏|粟田臣]]・[[小野氏|小野臣]]・柿本臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・阿那臣（吉備穴国造）・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣（[[武社国造]]）・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君（飯高県造）・壱師君（壱師県造）・[[近淡海国造]]ら諸氏族の祖としている{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 - [[春日氏|大春日朝臣]]・吉田連・[[丈部]] * 右京皇別 - 栗田朝臣・山上朝臣・真野臣・和邇部・安那公・野中 * 山城国皇別 - [[小野氏|小野朝臣]]・粟田朝臣・小野臣・和邇部・大宅・村公・度守首 * 大和国皇別 - 柿下朝臣・布留宿禰・[[久米氏|久米臣]] * 摂津国皇別 - 和邇部・羽束首 * 河内国皇別 - 大宅臣・壬生臣・物部 * 和泉国皇別 - 葦占臣・物部・網部物部・根連・櫛代造 * 右京未定雑姓 - 中臣臣 * 和泉国未定雑姓 - 猪甘首 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=天押帯日子命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[小野氏]] * [[和珥氏]] * [[春日氏]] * [[猪上神社]] （[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]） * [[小野神社 (大津市)|小野神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]小野） * [[多利思比孤]] {{DEFAULTSORT:あめたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:小野氏]] [[Category:和珥氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

（あまたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = [[孝昭天皇]]の第1[[皇子]] | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不明 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不明 | 没地 = 不明 | 埋葬日 = 不明 | 埋葬地 = 不明 | 配偶者1 = | 子女 = 彦姥津命 | 母親 = [[世襲足媛]] | 兄弟 = [[忍鹿姫命]] | 役職 = }} '''天足彦国押人命'''（あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「天足彦国押人命」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''天押帯日子命'''（あめおしたらしひこのみこと{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}）」、他文献では「天足彦国忍人命」とも表記される。『日本書紀』『古事記』とも、事績に関する記載はない。 第5代[[孝昭天皇]][[皇子]]で、第6代[[孝安天皇]]の同母兄、第7代[[孝霊天皇]]の外祖父である{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。[[和珥氏]]（和邇氏/丸邇氏）・[[春日氏]]・[[小野氏]]ら諸氏族の祖とされる。 == 系譜 ==
'''『日本書紀』に基づく関係系図''' {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|02| 01={{Supra|5}}[[孝昭天皇]]|02=[[世襲足媛|{{color|#FC4E6B|世襲足媛}}]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|^|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||!| 01='''天足彦国押人命'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01=[[押媛|{{color|#FC4E6B|押媛}}]]|02={{Supra|6}}[[孝安天皇]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}}[[孝霊天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}}
{{smaller|（名称は『日本書紀』を第一とし、括弧内に『古事記』ほかを記載）}} 『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』によれば、第5代[[孝昭天皇]]と、[[世襲足媛]]（よそたらしひめ、余曽多本毘売命）との間に生まれた第一[[皇子]]である。同母弟には日本足彦国押人天皇（大倭帯日子国押人命、第6代[[孝安天皇]]）がいる。 子に関して、『日本書紀』では娘の[[押媛]]が孝安天皇の[[皇后]]となり、[[孝霊天皇]]（第7代）を産んだとする。 == 後裔氏族 == 『日本書紀』では天足彦国押人命について、[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]（和珥氏）の祖とする{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『古事記』では、[[春日氏|春日臣]]・大宅臣・[[粟田氏|粟田臣]]・[[小野氏|小野臣]]・柿本臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・阿那臣（吉備穴国造）・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣（[[武社国造]]）・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君（飯高県造）・壱師君（壱師県造）・[[近淡海国造]]ら諸氏族の祖としている{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 - [[春日氏|大春日朝臣]]・吉田連・[[丈部]] * 右京皇別 - 栗田朝臣・山上朝臣・真野臣・和邇部・安那公・野中 * 山城国皇別 - [[小野氏|小野朝臣]]・粟田朝臣・小野臣・和邇部・大宅・村公・度守首 * 大和国皇別 - 柿下朝臣・布留宿禰・[[久米氏|久米臣]] * 摂津国皇別 - 和邇部・羽束首 * 河内国皇別 - 大宅臣・壬生臣・物部 * 和泉国皇別 - 葦占臣・物部・網部物部・根連・櫛代造 * 右京未定雑姓 - 中臣臣 * 和泉国未定雑姓 - 猪甘首 == 信仰 == [[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町小蓮にある[[式内社]][[小野神社 (南国市)|小野神社]]で主祭神として祀られている。伝承では、当地に来住した[[小野氏]]一族がその祖先神を祀ったことに始まる{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=天押帯日子命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[小野氏]] * [[和珥氏]] * [[春日氏]] * [[猪上神社]] （[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]） * [[小野神社 (大津市)|小野神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]小野） * [[多利思比孤]] {{DEFAULTSORT:あめたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:小野氏]] [[Category:和珥氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:dd]]'''Prince Ameoshitarashi''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]]]]

{{Infobox Japanese Royalty/Noble | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotara Shihime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, dates of birth and death unknown) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], mentioned in texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''. The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto," while the ''Kojiki'' uses "Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto." Other sources occasionally refer to him as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobihito no Mikoto." Both texts lack detailed accounts of his achievements. He was the first prince of the 5th emperor, [[Emperor Kōshō]], the elder brother of the 6th emperor, [[Emperor Kōan]], and the maternal grandfather of the 7th emperor, [[Emperor Kōrei]]. He is considered an ancestor of several clans, including the [[Wani clan|Wani]], [[Kasuga clan|Kasuga]], and [[Ono clan|Ono clans]]. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical chart based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|02| 01={{Supra|5}}[[Emperor Kōshō]]|02=[[Yosotara Shihime|{{color|#FC4E6B|Yosotara Shihime}}]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|^|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||!| 01='''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01=[[Oshihime|{{color|#FC4E6B|Oshihime}}]]|02={{Supra|6}}[[Emperor Kōan]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}}[[Emperor Kōrei]]}} {{familytree/end}}
{{smaller|(Names follow the ''Nihon Shoki'' unless otherwise indicated)}} According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]'', he was the first prince of Emperor Kōshō and [[Yosotara Shihime]] (余曽多本毘売命). His younger brother was Emperor Kōan (Ōyamatotarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto, the 6th emperor). His daughter [[Oshihime]] became the consort of Emperor Kōan and bore Emperor Kōrei. == Descendant Clans == The ''Nihon Shoki'' describes Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[Wani clan]] (和珥臣){{Sfn|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}. The ''Kojiki'' lists him as the progenitor of numerous clans, including: * [[Kasuga clan]] (春日臣) * [[Ono clan]] (小野臣) * [[Awata clan]] (粟田臣) * [[Kakinomoto clan]] (柿本臣) * [[Ichibiwi clan]] (壱比韋臣) * [[Dazaifu clans]] (羽栗臣, 知多臣) * [[Chita clan]] (牟邪臣, 武社国造) * [[Tsunoyama clan]] (都怒山臣) * [[Ise clan]] (飯高県造) * [[Kita Omi Kokuzo]] (近淡海国造) ... and others{{Sfn|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}. Additionally, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following descendant clans: * **Left Capital, Imperial Lineage**: Ōkasuga no Ason (大春日朝臣), Yoshida no Ren (吉田連), Takebe (丈部) * **Right Capital, Imperial Lineage**: Kurita no Ason (栗田朝臣), Yamagami no Ason (山上朝臣), Manno no Ason (真野臣), Wanibe (和邇部) * **Yamashiro Province, Imperial Lineage**: Ono no Ason (小野朝臣), Awata no Ason (粟田朝臣), Takebe, and others. * **Izumi Province, Miscellaneous Lineages**: Ashikumi no Ason (葦占臣), Mononobe (物部), and others. == Worship == [[Ono Shrine (Minami-Kochi)|Ono Shrine]] in [[Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku City, Kōchi Prefecture]], is dedicated to Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto. According to tradition, the [[Ono clan]] settled in the area and enshrined him as their ancestor deity.{{Cite web |title=Research on Historical Shrines - Volume 23 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=皇學館大学 |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=Historical Shrine Research Association}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary (Expanded Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}}} == Related Topics == * [[Ono clan]] * [[Wani clan]] * [[Kasuga clan]] * [[Inoue Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) * [[Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)]] (Shiga Prefecture) * [[Tadashi Hikoko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:dd]]'''Prince Ameoshitarashi''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]]]]

{{Infobox Japanese Royalty/Noble | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotara Shihime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, dates of birth and death unknown) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], mentioned in texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''. Other sources occasionally refer to him as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobihito no Mikoto." Both texts lack detailed accounts of his achievements. He is considered an ancestor of several clans, including the [[Wani clan|Wani]], [[Kasuga clan|Kasuga]], and [[Ono clan|Ono clans]]. == Genealogy ==

{{smaller|(Names follow the ''Nihon Shoki'' unless otherwise indicated)}} According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]'', he was the first prince of Emperor Kōshō and [[Yosotara Shihime]] (余曽多本毘売命). His younger brother was Emperor Kōan (Ōyamatotarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto, the 6th emperor). His daughter [[Oshihime]] became the consort of Emperor Kōan and bore Emperor Kōrei. == Descendant Clans == The ''Nihon Shoki'' describes Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[Wani clan]] (和珥臣){{Sfn|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}. The ''Kojiki'' lists him as the progenitor of numerous clans, including: * [[Kasuga clan]] (春日臣) * [[Ono clan]] (小野臣) * [[Awata clan]] (粟田臣) * [[Kakinomoto clan]] (柿本臣) * [[Ichibiwi clan]] (壱比韋臣) * [[Dazaifu clans]] (羽栗臣, 知多臣) * [[Chita clan]] (牟邪臣, 武社国造) * [[Tsunoyama clan]] (都怒山臣) * [[Ise clan]] (飯高県造) * [[Kita Omi Kokuzo]] (近淡海国造) ... and others{{Sfn|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}. Additionally, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following descendant clans: * **Left Capital, Imperial Lineage**: Ōkasuga no Ason (大春日朝臣), Yoshida no Ren (吉田連), Takebe (丈部) * **Right Capital, Imperial Lineage**: Kurita no Ason (栗田朝臣), Yamagami no Ason (山上朝臣), Manno no Ason (真野臣), Wanibe (和邇部) * **Yamashiro Province, Imperial Lineage**: Ono no Ason (小野朝臣), Awata no Ason (粟田朝臣), Takebe, and others. * **Izumi Province, Miscellaneous Lineages**: Ashikumi no Ason (葦占臣), Mononobe (物部), and others. == Worship == [[Ono Shrine (Minami-Kochi)|Ono Shrine]] in [[Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku City, Kōchi Prefecture]], is dedicated to Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto. According to tradition, the [[Ono clan]] settled in the area and enshrined him as their ancestor deity.{{Cite web |title=Research on Historical Shrines - Volume 23 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=皇學館大学 |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=Historical Shrine Research Association}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary (Expanded Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}}} == Related Topics == * [[Ono clan]] * [[Wani clan]] * [[Kasuga clan]] * [[Inoue Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) * [[Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)]] (Shiga Prefecture) * [[Tadashi Hikoko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] '''Prince Ameoshitarashi''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. Additionally, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following descendant clans: * **Left Capital, Imperial Lineage**: Ōkasuga no Ason (大春日朝臣), Yoshida no Ren (吉田連), Takebe (丈部) * **Right Capital, Imperial Lineage**: Kurita no Ason (栗田朝臣), Yamagami no Ason (山上朝臣), Manno no Ason (真野臣), Wanibe (和邇部) * **Yamashiro Province, Imperial Lineage**: Ono no Ason (小野朝臣), Awata no Ason (粟田朝臣), Takebe, and others. * **Izumi Province, Miscellaneous Lineages**: Ashikumi no Ason (葦占臣), Mononobe (物部), and others. == Worship == [[Ono Shrine (Minami-Kochi)|Ono Shrine]] in [[Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku City, Kōchi Prefecture]], is dedicated to Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto. According to tradition, the [[Ono clan]] settled in the area and enshrined him as their ancestor deity.{{Cite web |title=Research on Historical Shrines - Volume 23 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=皇學館大学 |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=Historical Shrine Research Association}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary (Expanded Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}}} == Related Topics == * [[Ono clan]] * [[Wani clan]] * [[Kasuga clan]] * [[Inoue Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) * [[Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)]] (Shiga Prefecture) * [[Tadashi Hikoko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Prince Ameoshitarashi''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. {{Infobox Japanese Royalty/Noble | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotara Shihime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, dates of birth and death unknown) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], mentioned in texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''. The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto," while the ''Kojiki'' uses "Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto." Other sources occasionally refer to him as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobihito no Mikoto." Both texts lack detailed accounts of his achievements. He was the first prince of the 5th emperor, [[Emperor Kōshō]], the elder brother of the 6th emperor, [[Emperor Kōan]], and the maternal grandfather of the 7th emperor, [[Emperor Kōrei]]. He is considered an ancestor of several clans, including the [[Wani clan|Wani]], [[Kasuga clan|Kasuga]], and [[Ono clan|Ono clans]]. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical chart based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|02| 01={{Supra|5}}[[Emperor Kōshō]]|02=[[Yosotara Shihime|{{color|#FC4E6B|Yosotara Shihime}}]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|^|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||!| 01='''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01=[[Oshihime|{{color|#FC4E6B|Oshihime}}]]|02={{Supra|6}}[[Emperor Kōan]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}}[[Emperor Kōrei]]}} {{familytree/end}}
{{smaller|(Names follow the ''Nihon Shoki'' unless otherwise indicated)}} According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]'', he was the first prince of Emperor Kōshō and [[Yosotara Shihime]] (余曽多本毘売命). His younger brother was Emperor Kōan (Ōyamatotarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto, the 6th emperor). His daughter [[Oshihime]] became the consort of Emperor Kōan and bore Emperor Kōrei. == Descendant Clans == The ''Nihon Shoki'' describes Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[Wani clan]] (和珥臣){{Sfn|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}. The ''Kojiki'' lists him as the progenitor of numerous clans, including: * [[Kasuga clan]] (春日臣) * [[Ono clan]] (小野臣) * [[Awata clan]] (粟田臣) * [[Kakinomoto clan]] (柿本臣) * [[Ichibiwi clan]] (壱比韋臣) * [[Dazaifu clans]] (羽栗臣, 知多臣) * [[Chita clan]] (牟邪臣, 武社国造) * [[Tsunoyama clan]] (都怒山臣) * [[Ise clan]] (飯高県造) * [[Kita Omi Kokuzo]] (近淡海国造) ... and others{{Sfn|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}. Additionally, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following descendant clans: * **Left Capital, Imperial Lineage**: Ōkasuga no Ason (大春日朝臣), Yoshida no Ren (吉田連), Takebe (丈部) * **Right Capital, Imperial Lineage**: Kurita no Ason (栗田朝臣), Yamagami no Ason (山上朝臣), Manno no Ason (真野臣), Wanibe (和邇部) * **Yamashiro Province, Imperial Lineage**: Ono no Ason (小野朝臣), Awata no Ason (粟田朝臣), Takebe, and others. * **Izumi Province, Miscellaneous Lineages**: Ashikumi no Ason (葦占臣), Mononobe (物部), and others. == Worship == [[Ono Shrine (Minami-Kochi)|Ono Shrine]] in [[Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku City, Kōchi Prefecture]], is dedicated to Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto. According to tradition, the [[Ono clan]] settled in the area and enshrined him as their ancestor deity.{{Cite web |title=Research on Historical Shrines - Volume 23 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=皇學館大学 |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=Historical Shrine Research Association}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary (Expanded Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}}} == Related Topics == * [[Ono clan]] * [[Wani clan]] * [[Kasuga clan]] * [[Inoue Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) * [[Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)]] (Shiga Prefecture) * [[Tadashi Hikoko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Prince Ameoshitarashi''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:original pages]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[ja:天足彦国押人命]] ]]

According to tradition, the [[Ono clan]] settled in the area and enshrined him as their ancestor deity.{{Cite web |title=Research on Historical Shrines - Volume 23 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=皇學館大学 |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=Historical Shrine Research Association}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary (Expanded Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}}} == Related Topics == * [[Ono clan]] * [[Wani clan]] * [[Kasuga clan]] * [[Inoue Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) * [[Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)]] (Shiga Prefecture) * [[Tadashi Hikoko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Prince Ameoshitarashi''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotara Shihime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, dates of birth and death unknown) is a figure in ancient [[Japan]], mentioned in texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''. The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto," while the ''Kojiki'' uses "Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto." Other sources occasionally refer to him as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobihito no Mikoto." Both texts lack detailed accounts of his achievements. He was the first prince of the 5th emperor, [[Emperor Kōshō]], the elder brother of the 6th emperor, [[Emperor Kōan]], and the maternal grandfather of the 7th emperor, [[Emperor Kōrei]]. He is considered an ancestor of several clans, including the [[Wani clan|Wani]], [[Kasuga clan|Kasuga]], and [[Ono clan|Ono clans]]. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical chart based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|02| 01={{Supra|5}}[[Emperor Kōshō]]|02=[[Yosotara Shihime|{{color|#FC4E6B|Yosotara Shihime}}]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|^|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||!| 01='''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01=[[Oshihime|{{color|#FC4E6B|Oshihime}}]]|02={{Supra|6}}[[Emperor Kōan]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}}[[Emperor Kōrei]]}} {{familytree/end}}

His younger brother was Emperor Kōan (Ōyamatotarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto, the 6th emperor). His daughter [[Oshihime]] became the consort of Emperor Kōan and bore Emperor Kōrei. == Descendant Clans == The ''Nihon Shoki'' describes Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[Wani clan]] (和珥臣){{Sfn|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}. Additionally, the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' lists the following descendant clans: * **Left Capital, Imperial Lineage**: Ōkasuga no Ason (大春日朝臣), Yoshida no Ren (吉田連), Takebe (丈部) * **Right Capital, Imperial Lineage**: Kurita no Ason (栗田朝臣), Yamagami no Ason (山上朝臣), Manno no Ason (真野臣), Wanibe (和邇部) * **Yamashiro Province, Imperial Lineage**: Ono no Ason (小野朝臣), Awata no Ason (粟田朝臣), Takebe, and others. * **Izumi Province, Miscellaneous Lineages**: Ashikumi no Ason (葦占臣), Mononobe (物部), and others. == Worship == [[Ono Shrine (Minami-Kochi)|Ono Shrine]] in [[Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku City, Kōchi Prefecture]], is dedicated to Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto. According to tradition, the [[Ono clan]] settled in the area and enshrined him as their ancestor deity.{{Cite web |title=Research on Historical Shrines - Volume 23 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=皇學館大学 |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=Historical Shrine Research Association}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary (Expanded Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}}} == Related Topics == * [[Ono clan]] * [[Wani clan]] * [[Kasuga clan]] * [[Inoue Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) * [[Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)]] (Shiga Prefecture) * [[Tadashi Hikoko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Prince Ameoshitarashi''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:original pages]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:Images]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] ]]

According to tradition, the [[Ono clan]] settled in the area and enshrined him as their ancestor deity.{{Cite web |title=Research on Historical Shrines - Volume 23 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=皇學館大学 |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=Historical Shrine Research Association}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary (Expanded Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary|2010}}}} == Related Topics == * [[Ono clan]] * [[Wani clan]] * [[Kasuga clan]] * [[Inoue Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) * [[Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)]] (Shiga Prefecture) * [[Tadashi Hikoko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Prince Ameoshitarashi''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] ]]

{{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotara Shihime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first [[Imperial Prince]] of the 5th Emperor {{ill|Kōshō Tennō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, elder brother of the 6th Emperor {{ill|Kōan Tennō|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th Emperor {{ill|Kōrei Tennō|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Chart Based on the Nihon Shoki''' {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|02| 01={{Supra|5}}[[Kōshō Tennō|{{color|#FC4E6B|Kōshō Tennō}}]]|02=[[Yoso Tarashihime|{{color|#FC4E6B|Yoso Tarashihime}}]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|^|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||!| 01='''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01=[[Oshihime|{{color|#FC4E6B|Oshihime}}]]|02={{Supra|6}}[[Kōan Tennō|Kōan Tennō]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei Tennō|Kōrei Tennō]]}} {{familytree/end}}
{{smaller|(Names prioritize the Nihon Shoki, with other names recorded in parentheses.)}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first [[Imperial Prince]] of Emperor {{ill|Kōshō Tennō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yoso Tarashihime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was Emperor {{ill|Kōan Tennō|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to Emperor {{ill|Kōrei Tennō|ja|孝霊天皇}}. == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Prince Ameoshitarashi''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first [[Imperial Prince]] of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Chart Based on the Nihon Shoki''' {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|02| 01={{Supra|5}}[[Kōshō Tennō|{{color|#FC4E6B|Kōshō Tennō}}]]|02=[[Yoso Tarashihime|{{color|#FC4E6B|Yoso Tarashihime}}]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|^|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||!| 01='''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01=[[Oshihime|{{color|#FC4E6B|Oshihime}}]]|02={{Supra|6}}[[Kōan Tennō|Kōan Tennō]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei Tennō|Kōrei Tennō]]}} {{familytree/end}}
{{smaller|(Names prioritize the Nihon Shoki, with other names recorded in parentheses.)}} {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first [[Imperial Prince]] of Emperor {{ill|Kōshō Tennō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yoso Tarashihime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was Emperor {{ill|Kōan Tennō|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to Emperor {{ill|Kōrei Tennō|ja|孝霊天皇}}. == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Prince Ameoshitarashi''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] ]]

The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]]

{{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]]

The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Link incomplete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]]

{{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Link incomplete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]]

The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]]

The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]]

{{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]]

In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]]

The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}.
