The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]]

He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Prince Ameoshitarashi-qq]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]]

The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] （天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa （天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010年}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Mikoto|2010年}}}}== See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * revision.text:
{{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}}

The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=draft}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}}

revision.text:
{{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=draft}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛|12=draft}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=draft}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=draft}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=draft}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=draft}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=draft}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=draft}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}}

His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=draft}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=draft}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=draft}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=draft}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=draft}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=draft}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} [[Category:New]]

His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=draft}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=draft}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}.
