Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=draft}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=draft}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=draft|12=draft}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}}

{{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=draft|12=draft}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=draft|12=draft}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=draft|12=draft}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=draft|12=draft}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=draft|12=draft}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=draft|12=draft}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=draft|12=draft}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]] {{citation needed|Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}}

The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=draft|12=draft}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]] {{citation needed|Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}}

{{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=draft|12=draft}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=draft|12=draft}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=draft|12=draft}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=draft|12=draft}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=draft|12=draft}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=draft|12=draft}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=draft|12=draft}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]] {{citation needed|Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}}

{{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]] {{citation needed|Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}}

revision.comment: 39 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:天足彦国押人命 up to rev 101636071
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 天足彦国押人命
（あまたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = [[孝昭天皇]]の第1[[皇子]] | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不明 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不明 | 没地 = 不明 | 埋葬日 = 不明 | 埋葬地 = 不明 | 配偶者1 = | 子女 = 彦姥津命 | 母親 = [[世襲足媛]] | 兄弟 = [[忍鹿姫命]] | 役職 = }} '''天足彦国押人命'''（あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「天足彦国押人命」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''天押帯日子命'''（あめおしたらしひこのみこと{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}）」、他文献では「天足彦国忍人命」とも表記される。『日本書紀』『古事記』とも、事績に関する記載はない。 第5代[[孝昭天皇]][[皇子]]で、第6代[[孝安天皇]]の同母兄、第7代[[孝霊天皇]]の外祖父である{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。[[和珥氏]]（和邇氏/丸邇氏）・[[春日氏]]・[[小野氏]]ら諸氏族の祖とされる。 == 系譜 ==
'''『日本書紀』に基づく関係系図''' {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|02| 01={{Supra|5}}[[孝昭天皇]]|02=[[世襲足媛|{{color|#FC4E6B|世襲足媛}}]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|^|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||!| 01='''天足彦国押人命'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01=[[押媛|{{color|#FC4E6B|押媛}}]]|02={{Supra|6}}[[孝安天皇]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}}[[孝霊天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}}
{{smaller|（名称は『日本書紀』を第一とし、括弧内に『古事記』ほかを記載）}} 『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』によれば、第5代[[孝昭天皇]]と、[[世襲足媛]]（よそたらしひめ、余曽多本毘売命）との間に生まれた第一[[皇子]]である。同母弟には日本足彦国押人天皇（大倭帯日子国押人命、第6代[[孝安天皇]]）がいる。 子に関して、『日本書紀』では娘の[[押媛]]が孝安天皇の[[皇后]]となり、[[孝霊天皇]]（第7代）を産んだとする。 == 後裔氏族 == 『日本書紀』では天足彦国押人命について、[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]（和珥氏）の祖とする{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『古事記』では、[[春日氏|春日臣]]・大宅臣・[[粟田氏|粟田臣]]・[[小野氏|小野臣]]・柿本臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・阿那臣（吉備穴国造）・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣（[[武社国造]]）・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君（飯高県造）・壱師君（壱師県造）・[[近淡海国造]]ら諸氏族の祖としている{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 - [[春日氏|大春日朝臣]]・吉田連・[[丈部]] * 右京皇別 - 栗田朝臣・山上朝臣・真野臣・和邇部・安那公・野中 * 山城国皇別 - [[小野氏|小野朝臣]]・粟田朝臣・小野臣・和邇部・大宅・村公・度守首 * 大和国皇別 - 柿下朝臣・布留宿禰・[[久米氏|久米臣]] * 摂津国皇別 - 和邇部・羽束首 * 河内国皇別 - 大宅臣・壬生臣・物部 * 和泉国皇別 - 葦占臣・物部・網部物部・根連・櫛代造 * 右京未定雑姓 - 中臣臣 * 和泉国未定雑姓 - 猪甘首 == 信仰 == [[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町小蓮にある[[式内社]][[小野神社 (南国市)|小野神社]]で主祭神として祀られている。伝承では、当地に来住した[[小野氏]]一族がその祖先神を祀ったことに始まる{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=天押帯日子命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[小野氏]] * [[和珥氏]] * [[春日氏]] * [[猪上神社]] （[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]） * [[小野神社 (大津市)|小野神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]小野） * [[多利思比孤]] {{DEFAULTSORT:あめたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:小野氏]] [[Category:和珥氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[天足彦国押人命]] to [[Prince Ameoshitarashi]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 天足彦国押人命
（あまたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = [[孝昭天皇]]の第1[[皇子]] | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不明 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 不明 | 没地 = 不明 | 埋葬日 = 不明 | 埋葬地 = 不明 | 配偶者1 = | 子女 = 彦姥津命 | 母親 = [[世襲足媛]] | 兄弟 = [[忍鹿姫命]] | 役職 = }} '''天足彦国押人命'''（あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「天足彦国押人命」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''天押帯日子命'''（あめおしたらしひこのみこと{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}）」、他文献では「天足彦国忍人命」とも表記される。『日本書紀』『古事記』とも、事績に関する記載はない。 第5代[[孝昭天皇]][[皇子]]で、第6代[[孝安天皇]]の同母兄、第7代[[孝霊天皇]]の外祖父である{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。[[和珥氏]]（和邇氏/丸邇氏）・[[春日氏]]・[[小野氏]]ら諸氏族の祖とされる。 == 系譜 ==
'''『日本書紀』に基づく関係系図''' {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|02| 01={{Supra|5}}[[孝昭天皇]]|02=[[世襲足媛|{{color|#FC4E6B|世襲足媛}}]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|^|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||!| 01='''天足彦国押人命'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01=[[押媛|{{color|#FC4E6B|押媛}}]]|02={{Supra|6}}[[孝安天皇]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}}[[孝霊天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}}
{{smaller|（名称は『日本書紀』を第一とし、括弧内に『古事記』ほかを記載）}} 『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』によれば、第5代[[孝昭天皇]]と、[[世襲足媛]]（よそたらしひめ、余曽多本毘売命）との間に生まれた第一[[皇子]]である。同母弟には日本足彦国押人天皇（大倭帯日子国押人命、第6代[[孝安天皇]]）がいる。 子に関して、『日本書紀』では娘の[[押媛]]が孝安天皇の[[皇后]]となり、[[孝霊天皇]]（第7代）を産んだとする。 == 後裔氏族 == 『日本書紀』では天足彦国押人命について、[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]（和珥氏）の祖とする{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『古事記』では、[[春日氏|春日臣]]・大宅臣・[[粟田氏|粟田臣]]・[[小野氏|小野臣]]・柿本臣・壱比韋臣・大坂臣・阿那臣（吉備穴国造）・多紀臣・羽栗臣・知多臣・牟邪臣（[[武社国造]]）・都怒山臣・伊勢飯高君（飯高県造）・壱師君（壱師県造）・[[近淡海国造]]ら諸氏族の祖としている{{Sfn|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 - [[春日氏|大春日朝臣]]・吉田連・[[丈部]] * 右京皇別 - 栗田朝臣・山上朝臣・真野臣・和邇部・安那公・野中 * 山城国皇別 - [[小野氏|小野朝臣]]・粟田朝臣・小野臣・和邇部・大宅・村公・度守首 * 大和国皇別 - 柿下朝臣・布留宿禰・[[久米氏|久米臣]] * 摂津国皇別 - 和邇部・羽束首 * 河内国皇別 - 大宅臣・壬生臣・物部 * 和泉国皇別 - 葦占臣・物部・網部物部・根連・櫛代造 * 右京未定雑姓 - 中臣臣 * 和泉国未定雑姓 - 猪甘首 == 信仰 == [[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町小蓮にある[[式内社]][[小野神社 (南国市)|小野神社]]で主祭神として祀られている。伝承では、当地に来住した[[小野氏]]一族がその祖先神を祀ったことに始まる{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=天押帯日子命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|天押帯日子命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[小野氏]] * [[和珥氏]] * [[春日氏]] * [[猪上神社]] （[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]） * [[小野神社 (大津市)|小野神社]] （[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]小野） * [[多利思比孤]] {{DEFAULTSORT:あめたらしひこくにおしひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:小野氏]] [[Category:和珥氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]] {{citation needed|Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto

Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]]

{{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{citation needed|Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto}} {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]]

{{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]]

{{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōan|qq=}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōrei|qq=}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|qq=}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōshō|qq=}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yosotarashi-hime|qq=}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōan|qq=}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōrei|qq=}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|qq=}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|qq=}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|qq=}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|qq=}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|qq=}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|qq=}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]]

Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|qq=}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|qq=}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|qq=}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|qq=}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]]

revision.text:
{{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōan|qq=}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōrei|qq=}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|qq=}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōshō|qq=}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|ja|世襲足媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yosotarashi-hime|qq=}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōan|qq=}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōrei|qq=}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|qq=}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|qq=}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|qq=}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|qq=}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|qq=}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|qq=}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]]

The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|qq=}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|qq=}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|qq=}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|qq=}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|qq=}} in [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]]. {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = [[Emperor Kōshō]]'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Yosotarashi-hime]] | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[Japan|Japanese]] imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of the 5th [[Emperor Kōshō]], elder brother of the 6th [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], and maternal grandfather of the 7th [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]] (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]] and [[:en:Yosotarashi-hime|Yosotarashi-hime]] (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]]. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|qq=}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|qq=}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|qq=}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|qq=}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|qq=}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|qq=}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|qq=}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|qq=}} in [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]]. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]] * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]]

The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Wani clan|Wani clan]] * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Wani clan]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:n most likely untranslated or similar]] }} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[sn:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[sn:rince Ameoshitarashi]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:和珥氏]] [[Category:Ono clan]]
