[[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Empress Oshihime|WD=Q11076797}}; {{ill|Hiko Wanabe Oshijinmei|WD=Q106379651}}|father={{ill|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841}}|mother={{ill|Yosotarashi hime|WD=Q10869205}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11443593]] Wikidata name: Ametarashihiko kunioshihito no mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime}} | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of the 5th {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}, elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|1=Kumano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Takebe no Asomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929666}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|1=Ono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464225}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{wikidata link|Q11443593}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Takebe no Asomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929666}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|1=Ono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464225}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{wikidata link|Q11443593}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

[[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Empress Oshihime|WD=Q11076797}}; {{ill|Hiko Wanabe Oshijinmei|WD=Q106379651}}|father={{ill|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841}}|mother={{ill|Yosotarashi hime|WD=Q10869205}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821}}}} {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime}} | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of the 5th {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}, elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|1=Kumano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Takebe no Asomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929666}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|1=Ono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464225}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{wikidata link|Q11443593}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

Both sources lack records of specific deeds. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|1=Kumano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Takebe no Asomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929666}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|1=Ono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464225}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{wikidata link|Q11443593}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

[[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Empress Oshihime|WD=Q11076797}}; {{ill|Hiko Wanabe Oshijinmei|WD=Q106379651}}|father={{ill|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841}}|mother={{ill|Yosotarashi hime|WD=Q10869205}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821}}}} {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime}} | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of the 5th {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}, elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|1=Kumano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Takebe no Asomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929666}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|1=Ono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464225}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{wikidata link|Q11443593}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:pages]]

The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Takebe no Asomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|1=Kumano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Takebe no Asomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929666}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|1=Ono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464225}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{wikidata link|Q11443593}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Wani Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{{moved from|Prince Ameoshitarashi}} [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Empress Oshihime|WD=Q11076797}}; {{ill|Hiko Wanabe Oshijinmei|WD=Q106379651}}|father={{ill|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841}}|mother={{ill|Yosotarashi hime|WD=Q10869205}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821}}}} {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime}} | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of the 5th {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}, elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|1=Kumano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Takebe no Asomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{moved from|Prince Ameoshitarashi}} [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Empress Oshihime|WD=Q11076797}}; {{ill|Hiko Wanabe Oshijinmei|WD=Q106379651}}|father={{ill|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841}}|mother={{ill|Yosotarashi hime|WD=Q10869205}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821}}}} {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime}} | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of the 5th {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}, elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|1=Kumano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Takebe no Asomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929666}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|1=Ono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464225}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|1=Kumano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Takebe no Asomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Takebe no Asomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami revision.text:
{{moved from|Prince Ameoshitarashi}} [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Empress Oshihime|WD=Q11076797|1=Oshihime}}; {{ill|Hiko Wanabe Oshijinmei|WD=Q106379651|1=Hiko Wanabe Oshijinmei}}|father={{ill|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}|mother={{ill|Yosotarashi hime|WD=Q10869205|1=Yosotarashi-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|1=Emperor Kōan}}}} {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime|1=Yosotarashi-hime}} | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese|1=Japan}} imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of the 5th {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}, elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=Emperor Kōan}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan|1=Wani clan}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime|1=Yosotarashi-hime}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=Emperor Kōan}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|1=Kumano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Q55529997|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan|1=Wani clan}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929666}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|ja|小野神社 (大津市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ōtsu)|1=Ono Shrine (Shiga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464225}} * {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|天足彦国押人命|version=101636071|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Prince Ameoshitarashi]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{wikidata link|Q11443593}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Imperial The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 {{moved from|Prince Ameoshitarashi}} [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Empress Oshihime|WD=Q11076797|1=Oshihime}}; {{ill|Hiko Wanabe Oshijinmei|WD=Q106379651|1=Hiko Wanabe Oshijinmei}}|father={{ill|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}|mother={{ill|Yosotarashi hime|WD=Q10869205|1=Yosotarashi-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|1=Emperor Kōan}}}} {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime|1=Yosotarashi-hime}} | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese|1=Japan}} imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of the 5th {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}, elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=Emperor Kōan}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan|1=Wani clan}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime|1=Yosotarashi-hime}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=Emperor Kōan}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|1=Kumano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Q55529997|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{moved from|Prince Ameoshitarashi}} [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] [[ja:天足彦国押人命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Empress Oshihime|WD=Q11076797|1=Oshihime}}; {{ill|Hiko Wanabe Oshijinmei|WD=Q106379651|1=Hiko Wanabe Oshijinmei}}|father={{ill|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}|mother={{ill|Yosotarashi hime|WD=Q10869205|1=Yosotarashi-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|1=Emperor Kōan}}}} {{distinguish|Tarishihiko}} {{infobox royalty | name = Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto
（天足彦国押人命 / あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと） | native_name = | house = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}'s first prince | title = | full_name = | Rank = | respectful_title = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = | spouse = | children = [[Hiko Omozu no Mikoto]] | mother = {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime|1=Yosotarashi-hime}} | siblings = [[Shikame Hime no Mikoto]] | positions = }} '''Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto''' (天足彦国押人命, あめたらしひこくにおしひとのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese|1=Japan}} imperial family member from ancient times as recorded in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}. The name appears as "Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto" in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, as "'''Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto'''" (天押帯日子命{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}) in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, and as "Ametarashihiko Kuniobi no Mikoto" in other texts. Both sources lack records of specific deeds. He was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of the 5th {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}, elder brother of the 6th {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=Emperor Kōan}}, and maternal grandfather of the 7th {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan|1=Wani clan}} (丸邇氏), {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}}, and {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, among others. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} According to {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, he was the first {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}} and {{ill|Yosotarashi-hime|en|Yosotarashi-hime|WD=Q10869205|lt=Yosotarashi-hime|1=Yosotarashi-hime}} (Yoso Tarashihime no Mikoto). His younger brother was {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=Emperor Kōan}}. The Nihon Shoki states that his daughter, {{ill|Oshihime|ja|押媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}, became the empress of Emperor Kōan and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} == Descendant Clans == In the Nihon Shoki, Ametarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto is described as the ancestor of the {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和珥臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wani clan){{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. In the Kojiki, he is regarded as the progenitor of several clans, including the {{ill|Kasuga no Omi|ja|春日臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōya no Omi|ja|大宅臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Awata no Omi|ja|粟田臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ono no Omi|ja|小野臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kakinomoto no Omi|ja|柿本臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakinomoto no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichihiwi no Omi|ja|壱比韋臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihiwi no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōsaka no Omi|ja|大坂臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Additional records name other clans, such as the {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|武社国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|1=Kumano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} and the {{ill|Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja|伊勢飯高君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Itaka no Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists his descendants as follows: * **Left Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja|大春日朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkasuga no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yoshida no Muraji|ja|吉田連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takebe no Asomi|ja|丈部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe no Asomi|1=Q55529997|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55529997}} * **Right Capital Imperial Clan** - {{ill|Kurita no Asomi|ja|栗田朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yamashiro no Asomi|ja|山城朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Asomi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wani no Omi|ja|和邇部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Various provinces** - Numerous lesser clans. == Worship == He is worshiped as the main deity of {{ill|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|ja|小野神社 (南国市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|1=Ono Shrine (Nankoku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18991294}} in {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}}, who migrated to this area, established his worship as their ancestral deity{{Cite web |title=式内社調査報告 第二十三巻 {{!}} 皇學館大学出版部 |url=http://shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp/book/detail/342 |website=shuppan.kogakkan-u.ac.jp |access-date=2024-08-28 |publisher=皇學館大学 |author=式内社研究会}}. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Ameoshitarashihiko no Mikoto|2010th year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ono clan|ja|小野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan|1=Wani clan}} * {{ill|Kasuga clan|ja|春日氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga clan|1=Kasuga Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513639}} * {{ill|Inoue Shrine|ja|猪上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Shrine|1=Igami
